issues.unsupported_claim: Horse has 64 chromosomes, donkey has 62, mule has 63.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference says 'mule = horse × donkey' under Reduced hybrid fertility but gives NO chromosome counts.
issues.suggested_fix: Either remove the specific chromosome numbers, or add a citation/source. If keeping the numbers, verify against a textbook — the reference does not vouch for 64/62/63.

issues.unsupported_claim: Horse (Equus caballus, 64 chromosomes), donkey (Equus asinus, 62 chromosomes), mule (63 chromosomes).
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Same as above — chromosome counts and Linnaean binomials (Equus caballus, Equus asinus) are not in the reference.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the specific chromosome numbers and/or the binomials, or supply a source. Reference supports only 'horse × donkey = mule, sterile'.

issues.unsupported_claim: Punctuated equilibrium proposed by Eldredge and Gould in 1972.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference lists 'Punctuated equilibrium (Eldredge & Gould)' but gives NO year.
issues.suggested_fix: Drop the '1972' date or cite it from a source. Reference only supports the authorship.

issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference says 'endosymbiotic theory (Lynn Margulis)' with NO year. The 1967 date is asserted in the outline as fact.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the '1967' year, or cite a source. Reference supports Margulis as author of the theory but not the date.

issues.unsupported_claim: Mitochondria from engulfed alpha-proteobacterium; chloroplasts from engulfed cyanobacterium.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference lists evidence for endosymbiosis (double membrane, circular DNA, 70S ribosomes, binary fission) but does NOT name the bacterial ancestors (alpha-proteobacterium, cyanobacterium).
issues.suggested_fix: Either generalize to 'a free-living bacterium' / 'a photosynthetic bacterium', or add a source for the alpha-proteobacterium and cyanobacterium identifications.

issues.unsupported_claim: Miller-Urey filled the flask with CH4, NH3, H2, and H2O and sparked it with electrical discharge, recovering amino acids in a week.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference says only 'Miller-Urey (1953) abiotic amino acid synthesis'. The four-gas mixture, electrical discharge, and one-week timeline are NOT in the reference.
issues.suggested_fix: These specific experimental details are widely cited in textbooks but absent from the supplied reference. Either trim to 'sparked a flask of gases representing early Earth's atmosphere → amino acids' or cite a textbook source for the gas composition and duration.

issues.unsupported_claim: LUCA inferred from the universal genetic code and shared core ribosomal machinery across all three domains.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference says 'LUCA ~3.5 Bya'. The 'three domains' framing and 'core ribosomal machinery' justification are NOT in the reference.
issues.suggested_fix: Either generalize ('the universal genetic code shared by all life') or cite a source for the three-domains claim.

issues.unsupported_claim: Specific dates for mass extinctions: Ordovician ~444 Mya, Devonian ~375 Mya, Permian ~252 Mya, Triassic ~201 Mya.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference lists the five extinctions by name and gives ~66 Mya for Cretaceous-Paleogene and ~96% for the Permian, but provides NO Mya values for Ordovician, Devonian, Permian (~252 Mya), or Triassic.
issues.suggested_fix: Either remove the unsupported Mya values or add a source. Permian ~252 Mya, Triassic ~201 Mya, Ordovician ~444 Mya, Devonian ~375 Mya are textbook-standard but not in the supplied reference.

issues.unsupported_claim: The asteroid impact at Chicxulub off the coast of Mexico ended the non-avian dinosaurs.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference confirms 'Cretaceous-Paleogene ~66 Mya, non-avian dinosaurs' but does NOT mention the Chicxulub impact or its location.
issues.suggested_fix: Either drop 'Chicxulub … off the coast of Mexico' or add a source. The K-Pg extinction is in reference; the impact-site detail is not.

issues.unsupported_claim: Cheetahs nearly went extinct in the last ice age; northern elephant seals reduced to about 20 individuals in the 1800s.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference mentions 'loss of variation reduces resilience' but gives NO species-specific bottleneck examples (cheetah, elephant seal) or numbers (20 individuals).
issues.suggested_fix: Either remove these two examples or cite a population-genetics source. The general principle is supported; the specific examples and the '20 individuals' number are not.

issues.unsupported_claim: Trilobite fossil record shows long stasis punctuated by rapid morphological shifts at ~500 Mya.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference describes punctuated equilibrium in the abstract but gives NO trilobite example and no ~500 Mya date.
issues.suggested_fix: Either remove the trilobite + ~500 Mya specifics or cite a paleontology source. The general concept of stasis/burst is supported, the trilobite illustration is not.

issues.unsupported_claim: Cichlid fish in Lake Victoria; >500 species diverged within one lake driven by female mate choice for male coloration.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference says 'sexual selection (cichlid fish in African lakes)' — does NOT specify Lake Victoria, the >500 species count, or the female-choice-for-coloration mechanism.
issues.suggested_fix: Either generalize to 'African Great Lakes cichlids' with no count, or cite a source for Lake Victoria and the 500-species figure.

issues.unsupported_claim: Galápagos finches on separate islands diverged into 13+ species with different beak shapes.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference says 'Galápagos finches' under allopatric speciation but gives NO species count (13+) and no mention of beak shapes.
issues.suggested_fix: Either drop the '13+ species with different beak shapes' specificity or cite a source. The example as such is in the reference; the numbers are not.

issues.unsupported_claim: Bread wheat (Triticum aestivum) is hexaploid, six chromosome sets from three ancestral grass species.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference says 'wheat is hexaploid' — no Linnaean binomial (Triticum aestivum) and no 'three ancestral grass species' detail.
issues.suggested_fix: Either drop the binomial and the 'three ancestral grass species' or cite a source. 'Wheat is hexaploid' is supported as-is.

issues.unsupported_claim: Habitat isolation example: garter snakes in water vs land. Gametic isolation example: sea urchins in open water. Behavioral example: bird songs (added to firefly).
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference lists prezygotic categories (habitat, temporal, behavioral, mechanical, gametic) and only gives 'firefly flashes' as an example. Garter snakes, sea urchins, and bird songs are NOT in the reference.
issues.suggested_fix: These are standard textbook examples but the reference does not cite them. Either generalize (e.g., 'two related snake species, one aquatic, one terrestrial'), or cite a source. Firefly flashes is the only example backed by the reference.

issues.unsupported_claim: Example of monophyletic group: 'birds + crocodiles + dinosaurs (Archosauria)'. Synapomorphy example: 'Hair defines mammals.'
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference defines monophyletic, paraphyletic, polyphyletic, synapomorphy, plesiomorphy but only gives 'reptiles excluding birds' for paraphyletic and 'warm-blooded animals' for polyphyletic. Archosauria and 'hair defines mammals' are NOT in the reference.
issues.suggested_fix: Drop Archosauria and 'hair defines mammals' if unsourced, or cite a phylogenetics textbook. The general definitions and the paraphyletic/polyphyletic examples ARE supported.

issues.unsupported_claim: Unit 7 sits at 13–20% of the AP exam.
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: The supplied reference is the Unit 7 content outline only — it makes NO claim about Unit 7's percentage weight on the AP exam.
issues.suggested_fix: Either remove the percentage range or cite the AP Biology Course and Exam Description (CED) where this is officially listed.

issues.unsupported_claim: Ribozymes are RNA enzymes (offered as detail for RNA world).
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: Reference says only 'RNA world' as a phrase — ribozyme terminology and the 'catalytic + informational' justification are NOT in the reference.
issues.suggested_fix: Detail is widely accepted but unsourced here — either trim or add a source. Low risk.

overall_assessment: The outline is broadly aligned with the supplied reference at the conceptual level — species concepts, prezygotic/postzygotic categories, allopatric vs sympatric, monophyletic vs paraphyletic vs polyphyletic, the four endosymbiosis evidences, and the headline endpoints (Permian ~96%, K-Pg ~66 Mya, LUCA ~3.5 Bya) all trace back to the reference. However the outline freely adds specific dates (Eldredge & Gould 1972; Margulis 1967; Ordovician 444 / Devonian 375 / Permian 252 / Triassic 201 Mya; Miller-Urey one-week timeline), specific numbers (horse/donkey/mule chromosome counts 64/62/63; >500 Lake Victoria cichlids; 13+ Galápagos finches; ~20 northern elephant seals), and specific species or sites (alpha-proteobacterium, cyanobacterium, Chicxulub, garter snakes, sea urchins, Triticum aestivum, trilobite ~500 Mya) that are NOT in the reference. Most are textbook-standard and likely correct in reality, but as a citation check they are unsupported and should either be removed, generalized, or backed by an additional cited source. Verdict: minor with several critical-flagged fabricated-as-fact dates/numbers (chromosome counts, 1972, 1967, Miller-Urey gas list).
